title: Gahlord Dewald

(born 1974) is a classically trained[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1) experimental musician[[2]](#cite_note-:1-2) and multidisciplinary artist. He is known as a bassist[[3]](#cite_note-3) and electronic musician.[[4]](#cite_note-:2-4)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

| Gahlord Dewald | |
| --- | --- |
| [Experimental double-bassist Gahlord Dewald performing with modular synthesizer](/wiki/File:Double_bass_and_modular_synthesizer_played_by_Gahlord_Dewald_at_New_Music_Gathering,_2017.jpg) Double bassist Gahlord Dewald performing with live modular synthesizer. | |
| Occupation | musician |
| Website | <https://gahlorddewald.com> |

**Gahlord Dewald** (born 1974) is a classically trained[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1) experimental musician[[2]](#cite_note-:1-2) and multidisciplinary artist. He is known as a bassist[[3]](#cite_note-3) and electronic musician.[[4]](#cite_note-:2-4)

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Gahlord_Dewald&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Education")]

Dewald has a B.A. from [Marlboro College](/wiki/Marlboro_College "Marlboro College").[[5]](#cite_note-5) He studied with [Salvatore Macchia](/wiki/Salvatore_Macchia "Salvatore Macchia") and [Mark Morton](/wiki/Mark_Morton_(double_bassist) "Mark Morton (double bassist)"), and participated in workshops and masterclasses with [Barre Philips](/wiki/Barre_Phillips "Barre Phillips").

## Professional life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Gahlord_Dewald&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Professional life")]

A [free jazz](/wiki/Free_jazz "Free jazz") experimental musician, Dewald has performed as a soloist on the Hawai'i Classical Performance Series at [Hawaiʻi Public Radio](/wiki/Hawaii_Public_Radio "Hawaii Public Radio"),[[6]](#cite_note-6) at the [Banff Centre for Arts and Creativity](/wiki/Banff_Centre_for_Arts_and_Creativity "Banff Centre for Arts and Creativity"),[[7]](#cite_note-7) and at [New Music Gathering](/wiki/New_Music_Gathering "New Music Gathering") as a featured soloist.

Dewald performs with Toussaint St. Negritude[[8]](#cite_note-8) in the duo Jaguar Stereo.[[9]](#cite_note-9) Other frequent collaborators include Jessica Ackerley,[[10]](#cite_note-10) rock band *Charles, Dead or Alive?* (named "7 Bands to Watch" in *Seven Days VT*),[[11]](#cite_note-11) [Greg Davis](/wiki/Greg_Davis_(musician) "Greg Davis (musician)"),[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1) Jabe LeDoux,[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1) Eric Segalstad in the band *Fat Tiger*,[[12]](#cite_note-12) and [Ariel Zevon](/w/index.php?title=Ariel_Zevon&action=edit&redlink=1 "Ariel Zevon (page does not exist)").[[13]](#cite_note-13)

As a composer, Dewald has written scores for films such as *Hanging in There* (2017).[[14]](#cite_note-14)[[15]](#cite_note-15)

As a [cinematographer](/wiki/Cinematographer "Cinematographer"), Dewald did the cinematography for *the sky in our hands, our hands in the sky* (2023), a single-channel installation directed by [Leilehua Lanzilotti](/wiki/Leilehua_Lanzilotti "Leilehua Lanzilotti").[[16]](#cite_note-16) Dewald has also worked as an animator, with credits on projects such as *7 to 10 Days* (2004)[[17]](#cite_note-17) and on the Dog Sharks series.

Dewald founded the Community of Sound[[2]](#cite_note-:1-2) in [Burlington, Vermont](/wiki/Burlington,_Vermont "Burlington, Vermont"), a non-traditional performance spaces with resources for [sound art](/wiki/Sound_art "Sound art") and production facilities.[[18]](#cite_note-18) He curated the *AM Frequency Series*[[19]](#cite_note-19) and *CoSine Series* as an extension of that project.

Dewald has been guest music faculty of the [Banff Centre for Arts & Creativity](/wiki/Banff_Centre_for_Arts_and_Creativity "Banff Centre for Arts and Creativity"),[[20]](#cite_note-20) [University of Missouri](/wiki/University_of_Missouri "University of Missouri"),[[21]](#cite_note-21) and [Lehman College](/wiki/Lehman_College "Lehman College").[[22]](#cite_note-22)

## Selected discography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Gahlord_Dewald&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Selected discography")]

### Label recordings

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Gahlord_Dewald&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Label recordings")]

* *ʻālohilohi* by Leilehua Lanzilotti on *forever forward in search of the beautiful*. New York, NY: New Focus Recordings, 2024.[[23]](#cite_note-23)
* Jessica Ackerley/Kevin Cheli/Gahlord Dewald. *Submerging Silently*. Richmond, VA: Cacophonous Revival Recordings, 2023.[[24]](#cite_note-24)
* String Orchestra of Brooklyn. *enfolding* by Leilehua Lanzilotti. New York, NY: New Focus Recordings, 2022.[[25]](#cite_note-25)[[26]](#cite_note-26)
* Dewald, Gahlord. *Coherent Light Enclosed Without*. Burlington, VT: Community of Sound, 2020.[[27]](#cite_note-27)
* Dewald, Gahlord. *Arousing // Fenestration*. Burlington, VT: Community of Sound, 2018.[[28]](#cite_note-28)[[29]](#cite_note-29)

### Compilations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Gahlord_Dewald&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Compilations")]

* *For Our Friends: A Disaster Relief Compilation*, “Strategic Game 0014c,” Aloha Got Soul,[[30]](#cite_note-30) 2025[[31]](#cite_note-31)[[32]](#cite_note-32)
* *RE:Natura #02—Ratio*, “Stellar Light Study #5,” RE:Natura, 2023[[33]](#cite_note-33)
* “Persica” on *Beats is Life*, Sonic Planet Recordings, 2021[[34]](#cite_note-34)
* *“He’s using time in order to serve space, I’m using space in order to serve time”* on *It Sounds Like Vermont*, Mount Pelier, VT: Histamine Tapes, 2018[[4]](#cite_note-:2-4)
* “Zealot” by Fat Tiger on *Sound Friends Vol 1*, Burlington, VT: Sticky Shed Tapes, 2018[[35]](#cite_note-35)
